name: Albert Jackson
bio: British author and woodworking educator, co-author of the definitive Complete Manual of Woodworking.

name: R. Bruce Hoadley
bio: American wood scientist and professor emeritus at the University of Massachusetts; leading authority on wood properties.

name: Christopher Schwarz
bio: American woodworker, editor, and author known for traditional hand-tool techniques and founder of Lost Art Press.

name: Bill Hylton
bio: American woodworking writer and editor at Rodale Press, specializing in router techniques and furniture making.

name: Lonnie Bird
bio: American furniture maker, woodworking instructor, and author specializing in hand-cut joinery and finishing.
top_work: The Complete Illustrated Guide to Shaping Wood

name: Ed Smith
bio: Vermont gardener and author who popularized deep-soil raised-bed vegetable gardening in North America.

name: Mel Bartholomew
bio: American engineer turned gardener who created the Square Foot Gardening method and authored its foundational guide.

name: Steve Bender
bio: Senior garden editor at Southern Living magazine and co-author of the Grumpy Gardener column.

name: Matt Lee
bio: South Carolina-born food writer and co-author of acclaimed Southern cookbooks with his brother Ted Lee.

name: Ted Lee
bio: South Carolina-born food writer and co-author of acclaimed Southern cookbooks with his brother Matt Lee.

name: Edna Lewis
bio: Virginia-born chef and author who elevated Southern cooking to a celebrated culinary art form.

name: Leah Chase
bio: New Orleans chef and owner of Dooky Chase's Restaurant, known as the queen of Creole cuisine.

name: Donald Link
bio: New Orleans chef and James Beard Award winner, author celebrating the rustic flavors of Cajun Louisiana.

name: Rick Bragg
bio: Alabama journalist and Pulitzer Prize winner; author of memoirs and stories rooted in the Deep South.

name: Scott Boatner
bio: Gulf Coast fishing guide and outdoor writer specializing in inshore saltwater species of the northern Gulf.
top_work: Inshore Fishing the Northern Gulf Coast

name: Patrick McManus
bio: American humor writer and outdoor sportsman whose essays about hunting and fishing appeared in Outdoor Life.

name: Rick Warren
bio: American pastor and bestselling author known for The Purpose Driven Life, one of the best-selling nonfiction books in history.

name: Charles Stanley
bio: American pastor, televangelist, and author of devotional and inspirational books published over five decades.

name: Jim Tolpin
bio: American woodworker and author who teaches traditional European hand-tool joinery and proportional design.

name: Waverly Root
bio: American journalist and food historian whose encyclopedic works on regional cooking remain standard references.
top_work: Food: An Authoritative and Visual History

name: Sam Venable
bio: Tennessee outdoor columnist whose warm humor and firsthand knowledge of Southern fishing and hunting earned him a devoted readership.
